symptomSummary: After a prompt revision accumulates ~30+ invocations, evolve regresses by addressing already-resolved issues rather than current failures, resetting only after a rollback.
rootCauseHypothesis: InvocationRepository.forRevision orders results ascending by date and caps at 25 rows, so once a revision exceeds that volume the telemetry window fed to evolve is always the oldest batch; recent thumbs-down signals are silently truncated before they ever reach the evolution prompt.
fixHypothesis: Flip the ORDER BY in forRevision from asc to desc (matching forArtifact) so the most recent invocations fill the telemetry window first; optionally pair this with a recency-decay weighting in the evolve telemetry-rendering step for additional resilience.

primaryLocation.rationale: forRevision uses orderBy(asc(date)).limit(25). Once invocation count exceeds 25, the query always returns the earliest rows. forArtifact at line 125 uses desc ordering correctly, confirming the asc on line 120 is an oversight.

candidateLocations.rationale: Calls forRevision and filters to failing telemetry with no recency weighting; a secondary decay layer here would add resilience even after the primary fix.
Alternative store implementation may carry the same ordering defect for non-SQLite users.
Telemetry rendering iterates in retrieval order; if upstream ordering is fixed but this layer doesn't trim to recent-failing-first, stale items could still dominate the prompt.
